findings.fix_prompt:
In Webflow Designer, open Project Settings (top right) → Forms tab →
Form Notifications section. Click "Add a recipient" and add
[email redacted] (or whichever inbox you actually check). Save.
Then go back to the live URL, submit the contact form once with a
test email, and confirm it arrives in your inbox within a minute.
Once verified, click Publish to lock the configuration in.
findings.title: Contact form silently drops every submission (no Form Notification set)
findings.what_we_saw:
On /contact, the form submits successfully (shows the green
"Thanks! We'll be in touch" success block), but no email is
delivered to any inbox. The form's action attribute points at
Webflow's standard endpoint; the failure is that Project Settings
→ Forms → Form Notifications has no recipient configured, so
Webflow records the submission internally but never notifies you.
This is the failure mode introduced by the November 2024 Webflow
update and is the single most common issue we see on live Webflow
sites.
findings.why_it_matters:
Every lead is being lost in silence. The visitor sees a success
message and assumes you got it. You see nothing. Most agencies
catch this only after a prospect emails them asking "why didn't
you reply to my form?"

findings.fix_prompt:
In Webflow Designer, open Pages (left sidebar) → click /about →
open the SEO tab on the right panel. Find "Search engine indexing"
and toggle it from "Disable" to "Allow." Save. Then click Publish
in the top right. After publish, reload your site
and view source to confirm the meta robots tag no longer contains
"noindex." Then submit the URL to Google Search Console for a
fresh crawl.
findings.title: /about page set to noindex, blocking Google from indexing it
findings.what_we_saw:
The /about page contains <meta name="robots" content="noindex">
in the head. This means Google and other search engines will never
index this page, even though it's part of the live site. The
homepage and other pages are correctly indexable. This pattern
usually means the page was set to noindex while staging and the
toggle was forgotten when you cut over to the custom domain.
findings.why_it_matters:
/about is one of the most-searched pages for any agency or studio
site (prospects Google "[your name] about" to vet you). Blocking
it from indexing means you're invisible to that search. It's a
silent SEO killer with a 10-second fix.

findings.fix_prompt:
In Webflow Designer, open CMS Collections → Blog Posts. Find
"Welcome to our process" and "Studio philosophy, 2026" in the
list. For each, either (a) replace the body content with real
copy and Publish, or (b) toggle "Draft" so the post does not
render on the live site until the copy is ready. If you have a
large queue of posts to draft in bulk, you can multi-select in
the Collections panel and Publish/Unpublish all at once.
findings.title: Two CMS blog posts contain literal "Lorem ipsum" placeholder copy
findings.what_we_saw:
In the /blog CMS collection, two posts ("Welcome to our process"
and "Studio philosophy, 2026") have their body field still set to
the default "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet..." placeholder text. Both
posts are published, so the Lorem ipsum is visible on the live
collection page and on the individual post detail pages.
findings.why_it_matters:
A first-time visitor browsing the blog will see "Lorem ipsum"
placeholder copy on the studio's own writing, which reads as
unfinished. The blog is the highest-trust signal on a studio site;
Lorem ipsum on it undermines everything around it.

findings.fix_prompt:
In Webflow Designer, open Pages → Home → SEO tab → scroll to
"Custom Code" → "Inside <head> tag." Paste this Organization
schema (replace the values with your real data):

<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "Organization",
  "name": "Bauer Studio",
  "url": "your site",
  "logo": "your site",
  "sameAs": [
    "https://www.linkedin.com/company/bauer-studio",
    "https://twitter.com/bauerstudio"
  ]
}
</script>

Save, then click Publish. Verify by pasting the live URL into
https://search.google.com/test/rich-results — Google should
report a valid Organization object.
findings.title: Homepage has no JSON-LD schema; Organization markup missing
findings.what_we_saw:
The homepage <head> contains standard Open Graph and Twitter card
tags, but no <script type="application/ld+json"> block. For a
studio / agency site, Google specifically looks for Organization
schema (logo, name, sameAs links to your social profiles) to
render rich results and to populate the right-hand Knowledge
Panel. Without it, your business shows up as a plain blue link.
findings.why_it_matters:
Rich results (logo in search, social links, established business
indicator) measurably increase click-through rates. Webflow does
not generate JSON-LD by default; you have to add it manually
via the Custom Code section on each page.

findings.fix_prompt:
In Webflow Designer, switch to the 478px (Mobile portrait)
breakpoint using the toolbar at the top. Select the services
grid container (Body → Section.services → Container.services-grid)
and either (a) change the layout from a four-column grid to a
single-column stack at 478px and below, or (b) reduce the
container's horizontal padding from 60px to 16px at the 478
breakpoint. Option (a) is the standard Webflow pattern and reads
best on phone-portrait. After the change, Publish and reload the
live URL on a real phone (or Chrome DevTools at 390px wide) to
confirm no horizontal scrollbar appears.
findings.title: Services grid overflows the viewport at the 478px (mobile portrait) breakpoint
findings.what_we_saw:
At 478px wide (Webflow's Mobile portrait breakpoint), the four-up
services grid on the homepage forces a horizontal scrollbar. Two
service cards extend past the right edge of the viewport because
their fixed width (240px) plus the container's horizontal padding
(60px each side) totals more than 478px. The 991px and 767px
breakpoints render correctly; this is a 478-only break.
findings.why_it_matters:
Mobile-portrait users (the majority of phone traffic) will see a
partial layout with horizontal scroll, which reads as broken even
though the content is fine. Webflow's three breakpoints are well
known for this: a layout that's clean at Desktop / 991 / 767 can
still break at 478 if anyone has forgotten to override.

passed_checks: dev tools and test data on the live site

share_metadata.description: Pre-launch audit. Verdict: Needs fixes before launch. 5 findings across trust, broken CTAs, mobile layout, and more.
share_metadata.title: LaunchLook audit for Bauer Studio

verdict.label: Needs fixes before launch
verdict.narrative:
Bauer Studio looks polished in Designer and at first glance on the live
URL: the brand work is strong, the Symbols are reused cleanly, the CMS
blog is populated. The Webflow-specific checks turned up the things
Designer does not warn about. The contact form silently drops every
submission because no Form Notification recipient is set. The /about
page is still flagged noindex from when staging was live, so Google
can never index it. Two CMS items contain literal "Lorem ipsum"
placeholder copy. Fix the form first (it costs leads every day), the
noindex next (it costs SEO every day), then the visible polish.
verdict.summary: Three quiet failures to clear before any client handoff.
